name: ftp-sftp-collector
description: Collects files from FTP/FTPS/SFTP servers with recursive directory traversal, pattern matching, ordering, completion checks, and post-collection actions.
author: hermes-core

settings_schema.title: Connection Settings
settings_schema.description: FTP/SFTP server connection and polling configuration. These rarely change.

protocol.title: Protocol
protocol.description: Connection protocol. FTP (plain, port 21), FTPS (FTP over TLS, port 990), SFTP (SSH File Transfer, port 22).

host.title: Host
host.description: FTP/SFTP server hostname or IP address.

port.title: Port
port.description: Server port. Defaults: FTP=21, FTPS=990, SFTP=22. Set 0 to use protocol default.

password.title: Password
password.description: Authentication password. For SFTP with key auth, leave empty.

private_key_path.title: Private Key Path
private_key_path.description: Path to SSH private key file (SFTP only). Takes precedence over password if set.

private_key_passphrase.title: Key Passphrase
private_key_passphrase.description: Passphrase for encrypted private key (SFTP only).

passive_mode.title: Passive Mode
passive_mode.description: Use passive mode for FTP/FTPS. Required when server is behind NAT/firewall. Recommended: true.

poll_interval.title: Poll Interval
poll_interval.description: How often to check for new files. Examples: 30s, 5m, 1h, 6h. Minimum 10s to avoid overloading servers.

connection_timeout_seconds.title: Connection Timeout (sec)
connection_timeout_seconds.description: Maximum time to wait for initial connection. If exceeded, triggers retry with backoff.

data_timeout_seconds.title: Data Timeout (sec)
data_timeout_seconds.description: Maximum time to wait for data transfer response. Applies to listing and download operations.

max_concurrent_downloads.title: Max Concurrent Downloads
max_concurrent_downloads.description: Maximum number of simultaneous file downloads. Higher values = faster but more server load.

retry_max_attempts.title: Max Retry Attempts
retry_max_attempts.description: Maximum retries for transient failures (connection lost, timeout). Uses exponential backoff.

retry_base_delay_seconds.title: Retry Base Delay (sec)
retry_base_delay_seconds.description: Base delay for exponential backoff. Actual delay: base * 2^attempt ± 25% jitter. Example: base=2 → delays: 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s...

retry_max_delay_seconds.title: Max Retry Delay (sec)
retry_max_delay_seconds.description: Cap on retry delay. Backoff will never exceed this value.

circuit_breaker_threshold.title: Circuit Breaker Failure Threshold
circuit_breaker_threshold.description: Number of consecutive failures before circuit breaker opens (stops retrying). Set 0 to disable.

circuit_breaker_recovery_seconds.title: Circuit Breaker Recovery (sec)
circuit_breaker_recovery_seconds.description: Time to wait in OPEN state before attempting HALF_OPEN probe.

host_key_checking.title: Host Key Checking
host_key_checking.description: Verify SSH host key on SFTP connections. Disable only for testing. Production should always enable.

input_schema.title: Collection Recipe
input_schema.description: Business parameters for file collection. These change frequently and are version-controlled.

remote_path.title: Remote Path
remote_path.description: Root directory to scan for files. Example: /data/incoming, /logs/equipment. Relative paths are resolved from user's home directory.

recursive.title: Recursive Traversal
recursive.description: Scan subdirectories recursively. When enabled, all files matching filters in all subdirectories will be collected.

max_depth.title: Max Traversal Depth
max_depth.description: Maximum directory depth for recursive traversal. 0 = remote_path only, 1 = immediate subdirectories, -1 = unlimited. Ignored if recursive is false.

folder_pattern.title: Folder Pattern
folder_pattern.description: Date-based folder pattern for targeted traversal. Only folders matching this pattern will be scanned.

enabled.title: Enable Folder Pattern
enabled.description: When enabled, only traverse folders matching the date pattern. Dramatically reduces scan time for date-organized directories.

format.title: Date Format
format.description: Date format of folder names. Examples: yyyyMMdd (20260316), yyyy/MM/dd (2026/03/16), yyyy-MM-dd, yyyyMM.

lookback_days.title: Lookback Days
lookback_days.description: How many days back to scan. 0 = today only, 7 = last week, 30 = last month.

timezone.title: Timezone
timezone.description: Timezone for date calculations. Examples: UTC, Asia/Seoul, US/Eastern.

file_filter.title: File Filter
file_filter.description: Rules for selecting which files to collect.

filename_regex.title: Filename Regex
filename_regex.description: Regular expression to match filenames. Only files whose name matches will be collected. Example: .*\.csv$, data_\d{8}\.json, ^report_.*\.xlsx$

path_regex.title: Path Regex
path_regex.description: Regular expression to match full file paths. Example: .*/2026/03/.*, .*/equipment_[A-Z]+/.*

min_size_bytes.title: Min File Size (bytes)
min_size_bytes.description: Minimum file size in bytes. Files smaller than this are skipped. 0 = no minimum. Useful to skip empty or incomplete files.

max_size_bytes.title: Max File Size (bytes)
max_size_bytes.description: Maximum file size in bytes. Files larger are skipped. 0 = no maximum. Prevents accidentally downloading huge files.

max_age_hours.title: Max File Age (hours)
max_age_hours.description: Skip files older than this many hours (by modification time). 0 = no age limit. Example: 24 = only files from last 24 hours.

exclude_patterns.title: Exclude Patterns
exclude_patterns.description: List of regex patterns to EXCLUDE from collection. Files matching any pattern are skipped. Example: ["\.tmp$", "^\.", ".*\.bak$"]

exclude_zero_byte.title: Exclude Zero-Byte Files
exclude_zero_byte.description: Skip files with size 0. Useful for placeholder files that haven't been written yet.

ordering.title: File Ordering
ordering.description: Order in which discovered files are processed. NEWEST_FIRST: most recently modified first (common for real-time). OLDEST_FIRST: oldest first (common for batch catch-up). NAME_ASC/NAME_DESC: alphabetical.

discovery_mode.title: Discovery Mode
discovery_mode.description: How many files to pick per poll cycle. ALL: every matching file. LATEST: only the newest file. BATCH: up to batch_size files. ALL_NEW: only files not seen in previous polls (requires state tracking).

batch_size.title: Batch Size
batch_size.description: Maximum number of files to collect per poll cycle in BATCH mode. Ignored in other modes.

completion_check.title: Completion Check
completion_check.description: Verify that a file is complete before collecting it. Prevents collecting files that are still being written.

strategy.title: Strategy
strategy.description: NONE: collect immediately (fastest, risk of partial files). MARKER_FILE: wait for companion file (e.g., data.csv.done). SIZE_STABLE: wait until file size stops changing.

marker_suffix.title: Marker File Suffix
marker_suffix.description: Suffix for marker/companion file. When strategy=MARKER_FILE, a file is only collected if <filename><suffix> exists. Example: .done, .complete, .ok, .ready

stable_seconds.title: Stable Duration (sec)
stable_seconds.description: When strategy=SIZE_STABLE, the file size must remain unchanged for this many seconds before collection. Typical: 10-30 seconds.

post_action.title: Post-Collection Action
post_action.description: What to do with the file on the remote server after successful collection.

action.title: Action
action.description: KEEP: leave file in place (use with ALL_NEW to avoid re-processing). DELETE: remove file after collection. MOVE: move to archive directory. RENAME: add suffix to filename.

move_target.title: Move Target Directory
move_target.description: Target directory for MOVE action. Created automatically if not exists. Example: /data/archive, /processed/{date}

rename_suffix.title: Rename Suffix
rename_suffix.description: Suffix appended for RENAME action. Example: .processed, .done, .collected

conflict_resolution.title: Conflict Resolution
conflict_resolution.description: How to handle name conflicts during MOVE. TIMESTAMP: append _yyyyMMddHHmmss. OVERWRITE: replace existing. SKIP: do nothing.

checksum_verification.title: Checksum Verification
checksum_verification.description: Compute SHA-256 checksum after download and compare with re-read. Ensures data integrity but adds overhead.
